items.title: Blur effect for dead bodies

posts.author: itzBlnky
posts.content: Hello! I’ve seen this cool system on Reddit where a player dies with a blur effect with this ModularScript from this dev forum post, but I do not know how to achieve this, and I’m hoping for some help from the community! There weren’t any other resources on any other forums, and it seems like nobody had thought of a solution. Their video:

posts.author: mra_oow
posts.content: Shaggy: You can make a UI and use https://create.roblox.com/docs/reference/engine/classes/Camera#WorldToViewportPoint On a few different points (2 points, Top-Left-Front, Bottom-Right-Back) to size the frame and apply the blur from the module you already found. You can calculate the position by getting the midpoint of the two points [ P1:Lerp(P2,0.5) or (P1+P2)/2 ] Where both P1 and P2 are the result of the output produced by the function referenced above. But, this may cause some unexpected results. So a better way to do this is to make 8 points, calculate 4 different sizes and select the one which is the largest. Next, to calculate the size, simply subtract the two points and you’ll get your size. Actually, skip this I’ll send you an example soon. I’m wrong.

posts.author: AmazingBarnabe
posts.content: could be done with a billboardgui, based on how the box remains in shape when the camera moves. Could use this, too. Just position the billboardgui right, blur it, and done. You can enable/disable the blur then using a remoteevent or bindableevent. Here’s a resource I found for the blur. It’s old, but I guess it’ll do: UI Blur [AUTOMATIC] Community Resources OFFICIAL FULL GUIDE VIDEO [Roblox GUI Frame Blur [2024] [UPDATED]] Example on how it would look like(old pics): [image] [image] [image] RECOMMENDED: Direct Blur Direct Blur + Blur Intensity Configuration Remastered Direct Blurring + Blur Intensity Atrribute Value [NEWST UPDATE INFO] The new update includes a setting for the blur intensity. local START_INTENSITY = 0.25 This is not supported on the module script and only for the new direct blurring version. [OLD UPDATE…

posts.author: mra_oow
posts.content: Right okay so you can use this logic: local Directions = {Vector3.new(1,1,0),Vector3.new(1,-1,0),Vector3.new(-1,1,0),Vector3.new(-1,-1,0), Vector3.new(1,0,1),Vector3.new(1,0,-1),Vector3.new(-1,0,1),Vector3.new(-1,0,-1),Vector3.new(0,1,1), Vector3.new(0,1,-1),Vector3.new(0,-1,1),Vector3.new(0,-1,-1)} local function Update() local maxX,maxY = 0,0 local i = 1 while i #Directions do local Dis = Camera:WorldToScreenPoint((Object.CFrame * CFrame.new(Object.Size/2*Directions[i])).Position) -Camera:WorldToScreenPoint((Object.CFrame * CFrame.new(Object.Size/2*Directions[(i%#Directions)+1])).Position) maxX = math.max(maxX,math.abs(Dis.X)) maxY = math.max(maxY,math.abs(Dis.Y)) i += 1 end local MP = Camera:WorldToScreenPoint(Object.Position) Frame.Position = UDim2.fromOffset(MP.X,MP.Y) Frame.Size = UDim2.fromOffset(maxX+50,maxY+50) end To size the frame and then apply the blur to that frame object. This is the code im using to apply the neon blur: neon:BindFrame(Frame,{ Transparency = 0.5; BrickColor = BrickColor.new("White"); }) The Result: image 408×462 20.4 KB image 264×338 43.5 KB It adjusts pretty nicely. And because everything updates frequently, you can use this to blur player heads! image 454×498 32.7 KB Kind of like Bodycam I’d say.

posts.author: itzBlnky
posts.content: Is there a way you could give me a step by step or an copy version? I’m not familier with ModuleScripts or applying them

posts.author: mra_oow
posts.content: I already gave you enough code, what I can do is give you a guide. Step 1: Create a ScreenGui and Insert a local script and a frame into it. Step 2: Open the script and paste in the given Update function and Directions table. Step 3: Insert the blur module into the script and make a reference to it in the script. Like local blur = require(script.Blur) Step 4: Use RunService.RenderStepped:Connect(Update) in the script to run the update function every frame. Step 5: Use the blur module to apply blur to the frame you just added.

posts.author: itzBlnky
posts.content: Sorry I have been busy with irl stuff but I hope I got it right but I’m like confussed about the last two steps, Step 4 and 5 Jack: Step 4: Use RunService.RenderStepped:Connect(Update) in the script to run the update function every frame. Step 5: Use the blur module to apply blur to the frame you just added. local blur = require(script.neon) local Directions = {Vector3.new(1,1,0),Vector3.new(1,-1,0),Vector3.new(-1,1,0),Vector3.new(-1,-1,0), Vector3.new(1,0,1),Vector3.new(1,0,-1),Vector3.new(-1,0,1),Vector3.new(-1,0,-1),Vector3.new(0,1,1), Vector3.new(0,1,-1),Vector3.new(0,-1,1),Vector3.new(0,-1,-1)} local function Update() local maxX,maxY = 0,0 local i = 1 while i #Directions do local Dis = Camera:WorldToScreenPoint((Object.CFrame * CFrame.new(Object.Size/2*Directions[i])).Position) -Camera:WorldToScreenPoint((Object.CFrame * CFrame.new(Object.Size/2*Directions[(i%#Directions)+1])).Position) maxX = math.max(maxX,math.abs(Dis.X)) maxY = math.max(maxY,math.abs(Dis.Y)) i += 1 end local MP = Camera:WorldToScreenPoint(Object.Position) Frame.Position = UDim2.fromOffset(MP.X,MP.Y) Frame.Size = UDim2.fromOffset(maxX+50,maxY+50) end And heres the screenshot of the layout I followed {844F508C-6983-4D1B-ACDA-F569BC656F93} 236×111 5.68 KB

items.title: Transparency for Drink Models

posts.author: ACherryPy
posts.content: As the title suggests, I’m trying to model some drinks for a game and I added plenty of details inside the cup itself, such as Boba Pearls, Ice, and a few other tiny things but if I make the glass see-through in order to appreciate the details inside, the details get all blurry and glitchy from different angles, but I also don’t want to set the transparency at 0 because I want to be able to see what’s inside. Image for example of what I’m working with: image 1074×929 107 KB Is there ANY way at all to fix this or get around this? Yes, both the liquid and the glass itself are separate models and so are the other details inside such as the pearls and the ice. I wish I could appreciate the cup and its details while still being able to see the liquid and appreciate the work i put in there. Otherwise going forward I’ll just have to make drinks with 0 details inside the liquid since it’ll look like a mess anyway. Anything helps. I’m a solo developer and this isn’t exactly my strong suit so any help and advice means the world to me. Regardless, thank you for your time and your patience, have a great rest of your day/night, take care. ACherryPy.

items.title: How to recreate old Roblox decal rendering?

posts.author: KauaBily2010
posts.content: Hi, I’m trying to recreate how decals looked in old Roblox. The problem is that in the current engine, decals react to lighting and look too bright, while in older versions they looked darker and more “flat”, like they didn’t really get affected by lighting. Old rendering made the outlines of decals appear thicker. In old Roblox, decals couldn’t be transparent (which is why the face in the first image is completely black) I don’t need it to be 100% accurate, but I’d like to get as close as possible to that classic look. Captura de tela_5-6-2026_21258_www.youtube.com 340×474 55.5 KB RobloxScreenShot20260605_212221408 1494×1153 72.2 KB RobloxScreenShot20260605_215859296 1494×1153 60.9 KB Captura de tela_6-6-2026_133923_roblox.fandom.com 196×235 20 KB I’ve already tried: SurfaceGui (doesn’t work well on curved parts) Texture (just tested it, didn’t really help) Stacking multiple decals (barely changed anything, just adds lag) Is there any way to make decals ignore lighting, or at least fake that old look?

posts.author: wiremass
posts.content: You can remove the light shine effect on a decal by setting the roughness map to pure white and the normal map to any image as long as it loads in. It won’t care to change roughness without a normal map specified. As for the pixel transparency being so rough, try a copy of the original image with the alpha of partially translucent pixels maxed all the way to become completely opaque. I don’t know of an image editor that can do that easily but I feel like NodeJS with a PNG library could handle that in bulk. I feel like I’m missing another detail from the old rendering. Is something else happening in the first screenshot?

posts.author: Godzilla_RealLife
posts.content: regardless of directly or indirectly contributing to the result you want, you can try using this object

posts.author: KauaBily2010
posts.content: That did not work, it only removed the classic shine, I want to keep the shine on the head, but have the face ignore it, SurfaceAppearance also requires a MeshPart, and faces do not stretch correctly on MeshParts, only on Parts with a SpecialMesh, here is how it looks now: RobloxScreenShot20260606_103042424 1494×1153 53.6 KB original face image 1086611 128×128 1.6 KB

posts.author: KauaBily2010
posts.content: Well, that is for the game lighting, if I change the lighting, it loses the classic lighting.

posts.author: wiremass
posts.content: I don’t mean using a SurfaceAppearance. The Decal class has both normal and roughness map properties for their image and it shouldn’t affect the original part’s look in spots where the decal was transparent.

posts.author: KauaBily2010
posts.content: So where does each image go? Roblox Studio Screenshot 2026.06.06 - 11.54.07.55 435×134 4.94 KB

posts.author: wiremass
posts.content: I use two images that are both just 1x1 pixels. The “NormalMapContent” property should be filled with a valid image ID. I use an opaque pixel out of habit and I don’t think the color matters since it gets roughed out but mine is #7f7fff if you want to copy me. The “RoughnessMapContent” should be set to a pure white image and I also use a completely opaque image for that. You might see the decal flash a bit before it loads in both of the maps.

posts.author: No1thatyoushouldknow
posts.content: I Believe The Property Is Called “LightInfluence” And You Just Set It To 0 Or -1, It’s Either In Texture Or Decal Properties

posts.author: KauaBily2010
posts.content: decal and texture dont have LightInfluence, just surface gui have it

posts.author: KauaBily2010
posts.content: I tried to do everything right, but it didn’t work, and it didn’t blink when I was adding the images RobloxScreenShot20260606_131024229 1494×1153 61.2 KB

posts.content: Light options for decals/texture (like GUI's) Engine Features The idea is for decals/textures to have light options that would either allow them to be affected by light or be static like GUI’s Here’s an example in my case: Made a brake light texture, the texture would be useful it imitated a light (GUI behavior). items.title: DistroKid Ingestion Failure - Multiple Albums by Daniel Bautista Missing (ISRCs provided)

posts.author: dbautistadecabo
posts.content: Hello, I’m Daniel Bautista. Some of my songs have been part of Roblox games such as Creeper Chaos, Left 4 Survival, Combat Initiation, Pilgrammed or Forsaken. I am reporting a critical failure in the music ingestion pipeline between DistroKid and the Roblox Creator Store . Despite successful delivery confirmation from my distributor, two full albums (30 tracks total) are completely missing from the library. The oldest one has been missing for over 5 months , which exceeds any reasonable curation timeframe. Asset Details ISRC Ranges: Album: Bestiarium (15 tracks) | ISRC : QT6E82553059 - QT6E82553073 | Sent: Oct 2025 Album: Archaeological Sessions Vol. 3 (15 tracks) | ISRC : QZRP52667733 - QZRP52667747 | Sent: March 2026 Technical Context: I am aware of the recent discussions regarding “Delisted” assets and curation delays mentioned by @AngelEthereal . However, these tracks are not even searchable by ISRC or title. I have verified that I own 100% of the rights and they are not registered with PROs (as per Roblox requirements). Roblox Support Ticket: #164299861 (No human response yet). Could a member of the Content Engineering Team verify if these are stuck in the manual review queue or if there is a metadata mismatch preventing the indexation? Thank you for your time.

posts.author: AngelEthereal
posts.content: Hi there, thanks for surfacing your concern. Can you please confirm that your albums on Distrokid have Roblox selected on the list that appears under “Add to more stores”?

posts.author: dbautistadecabo
posts.content: Hey, @AngelEthereal , thank you for answering! Yes, both albums have the ‘Submitted to Roblox’ option active. Screenshot_20260407_085836 1130×334 64 KB Screenshot_20260407_090517 1130×167 47.9 KB I have succesfully uploaded my previous 34 albums in the past. It’s just these last two the ones that haven’t been approved here, it seems.

posts.author: AngelEthereal
posts.content: Thanks for confirming! We looked into our system and found that there was indeed a bad ingestion attempt with your first album. We did a manual re-upload and you should now observe it on Creator Store (example: Praying Mantis track from Bestiarium ). We apologize that this album did not go through a re-ingest attempt until now and thank you for flagging this to us. Regarding your second album, there was detected similarity in certain tracks to previously distributed material. As a result, this album was not sent to Roblox since it does not align with the quality guidelines defined by Roblox. Please let me know if you have further questions or concerns.

posts.author: dbautistadecabo
posts.content: Oh, thank you so much for the re-ingestion of the Bestiarium album, @AngelEthereal ! So glad! About the last album, is there a problem with new versions of old songs then? I completely re-recorded (and re-mixed and re-mastered) some tracks used in Forsaken, Pilgrammed and Combat Initiation as a gift for my Roblox fans and a tribute for the Roblox devs who chose my music for their games the past year. Could you indicate me the exact guidelines which deal with that kind of new re-recording versions? Just to know and understand them and don’t send those albums to Roblox next time. Again, thank you for taking the time to solve the problem!

posts.author: AngelEthereal
posts.content: Hi again, it seems that your remixing matched against content that you do not own, even if you noted that you were remixing previous content. This is a detection done on DistroKid’s end; our guidelines are that, if a detection occurs, then do not send us the content. If you’re certain that you were only remixing your previous work, then I’d advise you to reach out to DistroKid instead; we did not receive the album and so cannot comment further on it. So sorry and wishing you the best!

items.title: How do I make this kind of SurfaceGUI Design?

posts.author: RohanArt
posts.content: Hello guys, how do i make these kind of SurfaceGUI Design? Reference: image 818×958 79.1 KB The design I made: image 534×723 46.2 KB I kinda want exactly the same of in the reference design p reciate it guys!

posts.author: KingAlijahforever
posts.content: Ayah! @RohanArt ! From the looks of it – its actually quite easy to break it down! So the initially is a wide frame, slightly curve with a uicorner on it – an paired with a uistroke, the background is simply a imagelabel. The numbers are textlabels, and the the part where it shows the users are imagelablels. posts.author: Reditect
posts.content: What I did for my game was a simple gradient, a png, square aspect ratio, say 500x500 pixels, and used Roblox’s Dynamic Clouds. I used Photoshop when I had it, but also www.getpaint.net/ , which is a free alternative. Know that a cubemap skybox is broken down like this: image 1444×1084 160 KB From Roblox’s documentation on skyboxes I only did the Up, Down, and a Side, assigning the Side to every other direction. Also, I found this tutorial that breaks down the process: Making Custom Skyboxes from Scratch Community Tutorials Making Custom Skyboxes from Scratch By: TheWinners152 | 2nd Edition This topic covers making your own custom skybox step-by-step with minimal tools that can be removed after creating the skybox. This tutorial is simple, and almost anyone can do it. It’s fast and doesn’t need high-end components, and did I mention it’s also free? Requirements Making the Skybox Gradient You will need Google Drawings , a free program developed by Google. If you would like to use another program to make the gra…

posts.content: Reditect: also www.getpaint.net/ If you use it, make sure to click this for download, there’s a lot of ads that are fake download buttons: image 843×615 151 KB

posts.author: Reditect
posts.content: The image itself is just a representation of the images needed for the skybox, not a template. Using it won’t work for the skybox. You have to upload individual square-ratio images for each face of the skybox. So, the Up part by itself, for example. Photopea should work, and any program that can make an image will work.

items.title: Realistic hair texture appears weird in studio

posts.author: ZombieKicker7
posts.content: Hey friends, I am working with a realistic hair mesh. Here’s how it looks in Blender. image 484×442 26.9 KB It is made with a principled BSDF and this opacity texture for the alpha. Nothing crazy. Hair_Transparency_Opacity 1920×1920 336 KB To import this into Studio, I bake the texture with these settings: image 300×242 5.86 KB And I get a result like: Nathan_hair_basecolor 4096×4096 6.15 MB When I import this into Roblox Studio and apply it to a SurfaceAppearance and set the AlphaMode to transparency, I get this terrible result, which looks bad and doesn’t have the color applied, why? image 417×349 36 KB Something interesting I noticed was that if I make the mesh have a transparency of 0.1 it then looks a lot better. image 363×343 20.2 KB This can be made even better if I duplicate the mesh and set the second one to have a 0 transparency image 534×464 30.8 KB image 331×352 17.7 KB Now the only issue is that the color still doesn’t get applied to it even though it’s clearly in my basecolor texture. Also note that changing the SurfaceAppearance’s color barely changes the color of the hair. How can I fix the transparency weirdness of the hair as well as get the color on the hair?

posts.author: vikitoooooo
posts.content: That’s because Roblox uses a lower step count for the AlphaMap when the object is at 0 transparency. I don’t know why the hell they do this, but it’s true that increasing the transparency makes it much more smoother.

posts.author: ZombieKicker7
posts.content: Do you know how I can get the hair color on the hair or improve the result of this transparency weirdness? Because right now, duplicating the hair would bring my tri count up a lot.

posts.author: ZombieKicker7
posts.content: Hey yall, my only solution to this problem was this: Significantly brighten the hair mesh’s color in blender or in a image editing software. image 384×193 61.8 KB Import the hair mesh Apply surface appearance with transparency AlphaMode Set the mesh part’s transparency to 0.1 Then duplicate the mesh part with the surface appearance and set this second one’s transparency to 0. This does unfortunately double the hair’s triangle count, but oh well. The result is something like this: image 481×434 29.5 KB

posts.author: vikitoooooo
posts.content: Don’t duplicate the mesh, what I do is open the image in any photoshop (would it be Paint. net, Gimp, or whatever that supports layers), duplicate the layers, merge them and export.

posts.author: ZombieKicker7
posts.content: I will try this tonight when I am back at my dorm thank you for the response.

posts.author: ZombieKicker7
posts.content: This worked! I duplicated the hair texture 4 times and it imported with colors and no weird transparency issues. Thank you! image 252×220 6.03 KB

items.title: Question About VFX Implementation

posts.author: exvirus2
posts.content: Hello, I was wondering whether this VFX is created using scripting or if it relies only on a Trail. I’m not sure how this effect could be achieved using a Trail instance. (You can see it in the video - sorry for the quality, it was recorded in Roblox.)

posts.author: IcyFragz1
posts.content: Hey! That would be a trail, with a decal on it. I am not a visual effects artist but that is 100% what this is. You can simply add a decal onto a trail to achieve something like this.

posts.author: exvirus2
posts.content: Yes, but what about a tapering effect, so the trail also narrows in width, and it gradually fades out from the end back to the start over time?

posts.author: ItsJustFir3
posts.content: You can use the WidthScale graph propety of the Trail object to achieve that effect, just set the endpoint to zero, if you want something where it narrows and then tapers off to zero like in the video you provided. Additionally, if you want to have it gradually fade out, you can also use the Transparency graph property of trails, set the endpoint to the maximum (1) if you want it to fade out completely, or something like (0.5) if you want it to remain opaque but still want the tapering effect to be visible. image 1006×856 8.84 KB Make sure to adjust the lifetime property to a high value like 1.45 if you want the trail to have a long length and duration.

posts.author: Skainted
posts.content: I managed to make a small prototype, the only missing thing would be to detect overlap and make it wrap around corners. Here is the code I’m using as of right now: local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local Params = RaycastParams.new() Params.IncludeInstances = {workspace.SplatBlood} local function GetBlood() return ReplicatedStorage.Test:Clone() end local function getHitFace(part, normal) local n = part.CFrame:VectorToObjectSpace(normal) local ax, ay, az = math.abs(n.X), math.abs(n.Y), math.abs(n.Z) if ax ay and ax az then return (n.X 0) and "Right" or "Left" elseif ay ax and ay az then return (n.Y 0) and "Top" or "Bottom" else return (n.Z 0) and "Back" or "Front" end end local GoalSize = 2 local FaceData = { Front = {u = "X", v = "Y", fu = -1, fv = -1}, Back = {u = "X", v = "Y", fu = 1, fv = -1}, Left = {u = "Z", v = "Y", fu = 1, fv = -1}, Right = {u = "Z", v = "Y", fu = -1, fv = -1}, Top = {u = "X", v = "Z", fu = -1, fv = -1}, Bottom = {u = "X", v = "Z", fu = 1, fv = -1}, } while task.wait(1) do local origin = script.Parent.Position local direction = script.Parent.CFrame.LookVector local spreadAmount = 0 local randomOffset = Vector3.new( math.random() * 2 - 1, math.random() * 2 - 1, math.random() * 2 - 1 ) * spreadAmount local spreadDirection = (direction + randomOffset).Unit local Raycast = workspace:Raycast(origin, spreadDirection * 100, Params) if not Raycast or not Raycast.Position then continue end local Part = Raycast.Instance :: Part HitFace = getHitFace(Part, Raycast.Normal) local SizeX = 0 local SizeY = 0 local Offset = Part.CFrame:ToObjectSpace(CFrame.new(Raycast.Position)).Position local OffsetX = 0 local OffsetY = 0 print(HitFace) local data = FaceData[HitFace] local rawX = Offset[data.u] local rawY = Offset[data.v] local SizeX = Part.Size[data.u] local SizeY = Part.Size[data.v] local OffsetX = rawX * data.fu local OffsetY = rawY * data.fv local Blood = GetBlood() Blood.Parent = Part Blood.Face = HitFace local AdjustedSizeX,AdjustedSizeY = SizeX / GoalSize, SizeY / GoalSize Blood.UVScale = Vector2.new(AdjustedSizeX,AdjustedSizeY) print(AdjustedSizeX,AdjustedSizeY) local AdjustedX = -1 * (OffsetX + SizeX/2) / GoalSize + 0.5 local AdjustedY = -1 * (OffsetY + SizeY/2) / GoalSize + 0.5 print(AdjustedX,AdjustedY) Blood.UVOffset = Vector2.new(AdjustedX,AdjustedY) end

posts.author: MoxnyStar
posts.content: What does your hierarchy look like in your UI elements? ウォーターゲーマープロ: i cant understand this anchor point stuff tbh Anchor point is the point where the object rotates, where its position is located, etc. Say you have the position at 0.5. With a normal anchor point, your frame will have its top left corner in the middle of the screen. With an anchor point of 0.5,0.5 your frame will have the exact middle of it also in the middle of the screen. Are you using aspect ratio constraints properly and making sure your UI is under group frames? By group frames I mean one invisible frame with an aspect ratio constraint that contains other UI elements, so that they stick together and can stay in one spot on the screen.

items.title: Can a cartoon animal have a butt dot

posts.author: Disgustedorito
posts.content: What do you want to achieve? I want to put plents from sagan 4 in roblox What is the issue? in “tailless” species, their nostrils are in the location of buttholes (they poop from their mouths). this would look exactly like the thing in some eastern kids media of cartoon animals having butt dots. (apparently this is considered “cute” in some cultures). I cannot figure out if this would be allowed. What solutions have you tried so far? A. I checked the community guidelines. I cannot find a rule that could even apply to it. B. I checked what western localizations of eastern kids media with that design element do. They don’t censor it. C. I asked other Roblox developers on Discord who didn’t seem to have an answer. D. I asked Assistant which said it’s “likely to be considered acceptable” but warned me to be cautious and suggested I reach out to Roblox support. E. Roblox support’s AI repeatedly reclassified my tickets as completely different things and rejected them for being unrelated to what it reclassified them as. help (please please be mature about this)

posts.author: uid_931
posts.content: No definitive answer is going to be decided because one moderator at Roblox can find this to look inappropriate and another won’t. The only answer that would be correct is if there was specifically a section in the Terms of Service that said what to do about this, but since there isn’t there won’t be a real answer. So as frozencold6 said, just don’t add it if you want to stay on the safe side.

posts.author: Blistent
posts.content: It’s an animal, it’s apart of their natural anatomy, I’d say it’s allowed depending on how detailed it is. If it’s not far from like a single black dot, then it should be completely acceptable. Roblox nowadays allows much more than what it used to. Things I’ve gotten moderated for 8-10 years ago (even warnings) now flies like the birds. This should be completely fine, but at your own discretion.

posts.author: Disgustedorito
posts.content: I mean, this is literally what the animal looks like image 1024×768 101 KB Also my understanding is that butt dots are a weird edge case that some platforms will be cool with and others won’t be. Hence why I chose to ask. For some context, Pokemon does butt dots.

posts.author: Disgustedorito
posts.content: Blistent: It’s an animal, it’s apart of their natural anatomy, I’d say it’s allowed depending on how detailed it is. If it’s not far from like a single black dot, then it should be completely acceptable. it would literally look like this in the game image 709×736 28.2 KB

items.title: How to make a text label that changes text glow

posts.author: idontknow395748
posts.content: I know you can make things glow using image labels but that wouldn’t work for my purposes because I’m trying to make a text label glow (damage UI), since that would change shape is there any way to do that or would I have to do every possible number manually. An example of this would be YBA’s damage indicators image 359×237 21.9 KB

posts.author: idontknow395748
posts.content: What I ended up doing is creating 4 positions (-, num1, num2, num3 so it appears as -123) and then manually creating - and 0 - 9 with a glow (I used Figma but anything will work since its just a .png) Then I split the number into its parts 1, 2, 3 and placed the correct image in the correct position, if there’s a more efficient way to do this please chime in local number = tostring(damage) local result = {} for i = 1, #number do table.insert(result, tostring(number:sub(i, i))) end local num1 = result[1] local num2 = result[2] local num3 = result[3] print(num1, num2, num3) local damageGui = script.DamageGui:Clone() damageGui.StudsOffset = Vector3.new(math.random(), math.random(), math.random()) if num1 then local firstNumber = script.Images:FindFirstChild(num1):Clone() firstNumber.Parent = damageGui.position1 end if num2 then local secondNumber = script.Images:FindFirstChild(num2):Clone() secondNumber.Parent = damageGui.position2 end if num3 then local thirdNumber = script.Images:FindFirstChild(num3):Clone() thirdNumber.Parent = damageGui.position3 end damageGui.Parent = character.Head task.delay(.3, function() damageGui:Destroy() end) image 182×425 8.05 KB It ends up looking something like this image 323×290 16 KB

items.title: [Full Release] The Future of Character Movement: Character Controller Library

posts.author: m0bsterlobster
posts.content: [Update] April 30, 2026 m0bsterlobster: We’ve updated the default positions and sizes for the touch thumbstick and jump button on all touch devices, including mobile phones and tablets! This shift improves ergonomics and accessibility for players while clearing the way for new default abilities coming in the future. This is automatically updated via the default player scripts. Since these control zones have shifted, we recommend checking your experience to ensure the new buttons don’t overlap with your custom UI. You can verify this quickly using the Studio Emulator or by testing on a physical device. Previously Now 345×172 22.6 KB 345×164 24.4 KB Character Controller Library Update (4/20/26) Hi everyone, Thank you for the continued feedback and support! We’ve just rolled out a minor update to the Character Controller Library with two important bug fixes and a structural change: Fixed: Addressed an issue where it was unintentionally possible to double jump. Fixed: Patched a vulnerability that made it possible for a client exploit to kill other players. Attribute Relocation: Ability Active and Enabled Attributes have been moved from Ability ConfigurationInstances to Ability/SyncedState. This architectural change was made to better support Server Authority. Please keep the feedback coming and stay tuned for some exciting updates! Best, Roblox Avatar Movement Team Key Takeaways The Character Controller Library is now available for use in live experiences . Switching between the legacy Humanoid and this new Character Controller Library is available via Avatar Settings. Go to the Movement section of the Avatar Settings and select the Character Controller Library to try it out! Hi Creators, Today, we are officially releasing the Character Controller Library for use in live experiences. This system, composed of the AvatarAbilities Library and the ControllerManager it was built on, represents a foundational shift: moving character logic and physics from the hard-coded Engine “black box” of the Humanoid into a transparent, performant, and extensible Luau implementation. Our vision is simple: enable natural and immersive character motion while giving you control. By moving to Luau, it is now possible to debug, configure, and extend character behavior just like any other part of your game script, without having to build a system from scratch or sacrifice performance. We believe the future of character movement on Roblox is Luau-based and intend for this system to become the new default for character control. No Changes to Existing Experiences: We will not change existing experiences and do not intend to deprecate any existing APIs. Template Rollout: Starting today, the Character Controller Library is defaulted to ON in the Classic Obby template . Over the next several months, we will progressively default it to ON in all Studio templates. Flexibility: Switching between the legacy Humanoid and this new Character Controller Library is available via Avatar Set

items.title: Upcoming Improvements to Physics Replication

posts.author: Khanovich
posts.content: Key Takeaways The improved physics replication system is now ready for early testing. This ensures eventual consistency to prevent lost updates during packet drops. Find it in the Studio Workspace properties as ImprovedPhysicsReplication . [Update] June 16, 2026 Khanovich: We’ve added a “Disabled” option to Workspace.ImprovedPhysicsReplication to allow games to opt out of this feature temporarily. Please note that this is a short-term workaround while we address ongoing issues, and it’s scheduled for removal as early as July 15. If you find it necessary to disable this feature, please file a bug report so we can ensure your specific issue is resolved. [Update] June 15, 2026 Khanovich: We’ve rolled out this update. Please note: the Physics debugging stats (as shown in the screenshot below) will be all 0s. We plan to ship a fix for this in the following week and will keep you updated here. Screenshot 2026-06-15 at 10.02.16 AM 346×399 37 KB Hi Creators, Starting June 15, we’ll be rolling out more reliable physics interactions in your games. You can opt-in now for early testing by using Workspace.ImprovedPhysicsReplication . We’ve significantly updated our backend replication system to provide: “Eventual consistency”, ensuring that updates are not lost due to packet drops Internal architectural improvements that will make it easier for us to support “Physics-like” Data Replication We provided a deep dive in a previous thread if you want to learn more. In our last update, we saw issues reported and rolled back the change. We’re continuing to refine how we test and roll out these updates so they stay invisible to creators Please note that once enabled, the network stats for physics will either disappear or drop to 0 KB/s. This is the expected behavior as traffic transitions to our new replication pipeline. Going forward, please use the stats for the new replication pipeline, Instance State Replication (ISR). This Update + Server Authority Do note that because Server Authority requires Workspace.NextGenerationReplication == Enabled as a requirement, this update has no impact on Server Authority games. Workspace.NextGenerationReplication is a more advanced version of this update, but contains potentially breaking changes (which is why it is not being automatically rolled out). What’s Next We plan to roll this out globally on June 15, and expect it to just be a silent bug fix to long-standing bugs. You can use this upcoming week as a window to flag any unexpected behaviors. Testing early allows us to actively respond to your feedback, whether that means delaying the global rollout or temporarily opting out of your individual place. Try it out and give us your feedback!

posts.author: Khanovich
posts.content: HealthyKarl: So, if I have a elasticity sphere bouncing up and down and I create a MicroProfiler snapshot on the server, it will no longer stop randomly? What you’re describing sounds like a performance hang that causes updates to not be sent. This will still happen. Eventual consistency simply means that when the part comes to rest, we will ALWAYS make sure the final update arrives on all clients. This is not true on current Roblox, as we will try to send the final update a few times, but if you have a connection interruption or higher priority updates to send the part may come to rest in the wrong location.

posts.author: Fliktem
posts.content: Khanovich: We’re continuing to refine how we test and roll out these updates so they stay invisible to creators Invisible? That’s a bit odd, isn’t it? That’s not what I meant… Heads Up: Upcoming Rollout of Improved Physics Replication! Ideally it should go in the regular announcements category.

posts.author: Khanovich
posts.content: Fliktem: Invisible? That’s a bit odd, isn’t it? That’s not what I meant… Bad wording (on my part), when we say “invisible” we mean tech changes that should not impact behavior do not actually change behavior and don’t bother developers. The fact that there were any bugs is the visibility that we’re talking about. We’re not trying to hide that changes happened.

posts.author: PeZsmistic
posts.content: What’s the goal / what do you have in mind for the note about physics-like data replication?

posts.author: Khanovich
posts.content: Right now Physics Data is the only thing optimized for synchronizing continuously changing Game State, because Physics Data for non-sleeping parts is almost always changing every frame. All other data, like the Color, Anchored Boolean, everything on the properties list is optimized for “Discrete” updates which is great if you’re making one off changes but is bad if you’re changing them every frame and expecting them to synchronize. Whenever we’ve wanted to add new continuously changing data (think of adding bone positions/offsets for animation that happens during simulation), we’ve never been able to do it because the Physics Sender was hyper optimized for sending ONLY Position Velocity data, and wouldn’t be able to add other data into it. The architecture lets us really easily just mark a property as “Continuous” and send it via the new system, which means there is more freedom to what kind of Properties we can add and keep synchronized.

So now that we’ve fixed all those bugs, we’re trying to give creators the option to opt into this early and tell us if there are any issues we didn’t catch. posts.author: BHNoire
posts.content: looks pretty good. previously, jumping and landing would almost always cause a resimulation and the same would be true for sudden velocity changes, even from things as simple as setting AssemblyLinearVelocity to 0 (this was very troubling when figuring out why landing would cause a resim). there are still some minor cases with collisions (particularly via geometry that isn’t uniform) and erratic player movement, but that’s to be expected honestly. Internal architectural improvements that will make it easier for us to support “Physics-like” Data Replication all i have to say is that i hope this is at least some progress towards calling .Destroy() on simulated instances because lord help me if i have to figure out how to make constraint movers not rollback when disabled one more time i might have to be admitted to arkham asylum “Eventual consistency”, ensuring that updates are not lost due to packet drops this one in particular was very huge. prior to this, seemingly-perfect resimulations would end up leading to rollback on top of rollback which would even leak into the visual entity renderer for my game depending on the circumstance. this forced me to making assumptions about the play-state regarding sequenced things (particularly framedata), like assuming that a sequence should waive rollbacks as long as the sequence’s initialization itself wasn’t rollbacked underneath strict termination guidelines (e.g. air movement is constrained over time via coded-in parabolic motion). the problem is still absolutely apparent, but far muted in many circumstances compared to before. unsure if this is what the update targets, but i did notice improvements in these areas (specifically: applying knockback to entities) - especially when joining lobbies with 80ms ping. to sum it up, most of the things affected once caused me many a headache when trying to maintain smoothness when a bunch of random irrelevant resimulations occurred at the same time. hopefully, this will continue to show a reduction in headache from filtering out the noise from code-related mispredictions (cough, mover constraints…) and “weird physics inaccuracies.” that being said, my before/after below, with network settings (note packet loss jitter, track visible resims via the gauge on the left of the character + general character animation) image 315×174 2.1 KB before: after: edit: oh my goodness just look at the lack of rollbacks logs in the after video its so perfect

items.title: Creator Roadmap 2026: Spring Update

posts.author: Roblox
posts.content: Key Takeaways In our Spring Creator Roadmap update, we shipped 52 items and are adding 21 new items. Read the post for information on what launched, what’s coming, and what’s been delayed. Hi Creators, With spring in full swing, we’re sharing our latest Creator Roadmap update. We’ve launched a ton of new features; while you can find a full list of what we launched below, let’s recap some of the highlights. Pushing the Boundaries of Roblox’s Engine You asked, and we delivered. Whether you’re creating in the classic Roblox style or building the next novel game , we want our engine to help you realize your vision. Visual Glow and Detail: Emissive masks are now available for you to make your worlds glow. Roblox now also supports 4K Textures for highly detailed and complex models. Realistic Soundscapes: Acoustic Simulation includes real-time occlusion and reverb, ensuring your audio environments react dynamically to the physical space of your games. image4 1449×787 96.8 KB image1 349×550 77 KB Bringing Your Idea to Life Faster With Assistant As we’ve seen more creators use Assistant, we’re starting to see just how much it accelerates creation and helps eliminate the pressure of crunch time. We’ve upgraded Assistant in many ways, but let’s cover the latest: Planning Mode: Assistant now has a planning mode that generates a task list based on your prompt, lets you review and tweak the steps, executes the work, and then validates the output with automated testing. Procedural Models: With the new /generate_procedural_model command, you can instantly create procedural models that are highly configurable. Just by adjusting a parameter, you can change a procedural model’s geometry, color, or material in seconds. Also, for the AI power users: Studio now has MCP support built-in and you can use your own key with external LLMs from Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI. Advancing Avatar Creation We also launched new features to expand Avatar fidelity: Full Body Animation for Platform Avatars: R15 platform avatars now support higher fidelity animation rigs such as articulated fingers, a refined torso with clavicles, and ball-of-foot joints. High-Fidelity Visuals: We launched Makeup , up-rezed textures to 2K , and upgraded joints for smoother movement. Advanced Animation: Animation Graphs are now available as a Studio Beta for seamless transitions and blends, alongside the Character Controller library. image2 1200×675 80.1 KB Upcoming New Tech While you can read a full list of what we added below, here are some highlights of what’s to come. In the coming months we’re: Exposing Open Cloud APIs as tools available to Assistant and MCP (Mid 2026), which lets you bring the power of Roblox directly into external tools like Cursor and Claude Code. Introducing some exciting UI improvements with StyleQueries (Mid 2026) and native UI shadows and glows (Mid 2026) to make developing high quality UIs easier. Tackling Spammy and Cloned Games Before we wrap up, let’s talk about a major

items.title: Simulating thousands of moving NPCs with humanoids/physics performantly

posts.author: me7474
posts.content: Table of Contents Introduction Getting started New enemy function bitpacking writing a bitpacker Networking architecture (server) Networking architecture (client) Client rendering Server handling Showcase Closing thoughts the table of contents does not work right now, 0 clue what I did wrong. I am tired and will fix it in the morning. Introduction Around a year ago I was looking for a way to simulate large amounts of NPCs without significant server/client lag or latency due to high network usage. For my purposes these NPCs needed to maintain their humanoids so they could interact with unanchored parts using the built in physics system. I eventually arrived at an implementation that took me from 30 NPCs causing me significant server lag (running default humanoids on the server) to being able to run 500 NPCs with an average of ~46 kb/s received networking data on client, or 1000 NPCs with ~112 kb/s. At these numbers the greatest constraint is the device the client is playing on, I found anything not high end struggled far before 500 due to all my NPCs moving and being animated. This tutorial will be kept somewhat non specific, I will be covering architecture and networking in detail but to truly achieve whatever you want to do with your NPCs you will likely need some sort of state machine or behavior controller giving your NPCs actual logic. There is ample support for this should you wish to implement it however it is outside the scope of what I wish to cover (perhaps in a part two ). Special shoutout to @Devalvion and his Calculon series for inspiring me to finally write up a post. Highly recommend his work, very well thought out and technically impressive topics being explained. Getting started In order to fully optimize your NPCs you need to have a Server NPC and a Client NPC . The server NPC is a barebones hitbox that solely exists to simulate physics pictured below: image 726×370 38.5 KB and the client NPC is the visual aspect of the system: image 754×498 72.7 KB You’ll want to make the server NPC the dimensions of the physics hitbox you want for your NPC. In my case you will see it is the same height as the Noob, however it is only two studs wide as I did not want the NPC to have arm collisions. Place these rigs wherever you want, but make sure the client NPC is somewhere the client can access. In my case I put them both in replicated storage. New enemy function The architecture I used requires a server script, local script, and module script. You also need a remote event to send enemy data to clients. I placed this remote along with the module script in replicated storage, the server script in server script service, and the local script in starter player scripts. Finally I created a folder called “Enemies” located under workspace. Let’s go over how this system actually works before we make it. When an NPC is created we spawn the server rig on the server and crucially don’t replicate it. For most applications the majority of built in humanoid

posts.author: me7474
posts.content: what have you done clientside to optimize? I feel I am much better at networking optimization than I am rendering and have always found FPS to be the limiting factor lol. My most recent version of this system I am able to get 1,750 NPCs at ~60 FPS with ~100 kb/s receive (notable spikes at NPC creation/moving a bunch in the same “tick” though with me defining every “tick” as the interval at which packets send). I am confident I could probably push it to 2-3k npcs if the clientside wasn’t a limiting factor and at this point I am just trying to see how far I can take it lol

posts.author: aC_asual
posts.content: I disabled most humanoid features such as states and the nametags. Script wise i made it so that interpolation uses multiple actors and scripts, moving also uses BulkMoveTo.

posts.author: DadHobbitWizard
posts.content: Great system. This is very similar to how it’s done in optimized multiplayer services. I have had to do this in an engine called Shiva, but had to use packed strings because it only had basic data types! Great thought around only sending ROT Y. If the terrain is flat, that’s 3 more not needed too. One further optimization is skipping the object if it hasn’t materially changed state, and then sending master state every X seconds for new joiners. You can also optimize for distance from nearest player if not visible, to reduce ticks. The client interpolation can have more varieties too, depending on how accurate it needs to be. Love to see this kind of stuff! Roblox is versatile

posts.author: me7474
posts.content: In my most recent version of this system I only send changed NPCs, update non critical properties less often than every tenth of a second, and have been playing around with a pooling system I haven’t seen anywhere else. I swapped the remotes to send delta changes for positions instead of full positions, because I am firing every 0.1 seconds there is only so many deltas that will actually be seen per packet. Most of these numbers are duplicates which means at the expense of simplicity you can cut down greatly on network. Basically instead of assigning each NPC a buffer I assign NPC IDs to delta values. Say 150 NPCs had a delta X position of +3, I make a long buffer with all 150 IDs. I know each ID is 11 bits so I don’t need to provide any separators. the packet for this would look like so: {bufferForXDeltas, {bufferForDeltaOne, bufferForDeltaTwo, bufferForDeltaThree, ...}, ...} say my X deltas for this packet are +2, +4, -1. I would include three buffers with IDs that match the deltas, so a psuedocode packet that represents the same structure would look like this: {{2, 4, -1}, {{ID_4, ID_7}, {ID_3}, {ID_1, ID_12, ID_6, ID_5}}, ...} Now I know NPC 4 and 7 have a delta X of +2, NPC 3 a delta of +4, etc. and because I am using deltas and sending frequently there aren’t many X deltas so we cut a ton of deadweight loss attributed to repeating them. I also have thought about replacing the ID buffer containers with a single bit for each NPC given I know the amount of existing NPCs. If I have 8 NPCs alive and ID 2 and 6 have a delta of +1 I can set the +1 key to 01000100 , with the ones corresponding to the IDs that change. I also considered replacing the value keys with binary addition. If we have NPCs that move -1, 0, 1, 2 in a packet we could use half the data by sending a reference saying “hey we are starting at -1” and then only having fields for +1 and +2 as these deltas can form any of the values above. At the end of the day these are just pushing it for the sake of pushing it though, my client performance is already the limiting factor so 0 reason to improve networking I am unsure if these concepts has been done before but I personally couldn’t find anything like them trying to look it up. Considering you have been in the gamedev scene a lot longer, do you know if this is an original set of concepts or commonly used? edit: also worth noting obviously using deltas has the added benefit of needing to allocate less bits per number. The way I make new NPCs/load NPCs for joining players also still uses my old packet system with full info but considering it’s a one time send per NPC it is minimally impactful.

posts.author: DadHobbitWizard
posts.content: That’s clever. Haven’t heard of that specific optimization! Very cool. Pools is good too, we used to do something similar in Asheron’s call in dynamically sized vertical server bands depending on player clustering.

posts.author: aC_asual
posts.content: @me7474 Do you have any tips for using Pathfinding (custom or not) with this large amount of AI?

posts.author: me7474
posts.content: it depends on what usecase you have but if you want them all pathfinding individually with non reused paths that is pretty much out of the question unfortunately. I haven’t looked into pathfinding much but off the top of my head the first two things I would look at would be entity grouping (e.g. if you have 50 enemies all clumped together you want to move from A to B you can basically treat them as a single entity, pathfind for only one and move them as a hoard) and caching of paths (if you expect NPCs to be traveling the same path multiple times save the path so you don’t have to recalculate). It also heavily depends on what you are trying to pathfind across. I’ve done A* algorithm implementations for when I had a terrain system that could be categorized as a grid, for more complex maps though you will likely have to come up with your own solutions

posts.author: aC_asual
posts.content: Okay so, i couldnt really find any good alternatives to Pathfinding (since i want the AI to work in a 3D environment with multiple height levels and other factors), so i resorted to using SimplePath , which turned out pretty well! Managed to get 250 Pathfinding AI without any issues. There’s still alot of room for improvement, but so far everything’s working as intended. As a side note, while messing around with AgentRadius and a Waypoint-Skip mechanic, they created this “crowding” effect where they all clump up and it really sells into the hivemind feeling of these zombies.

posts.author: me7474
posts.content: wow, I’m surprised it runs so efficiently with 250! I fully expected built in pathfinding to be too expensive to do per npc for anything above like maybe 50-100, that is great though!

posts.author: vantoanvh_dev
posts.content: I have tried to make a system, but will be in the future as a resource. This is like the most impossible thing you can think of simulating 500 NPCs performantly. or even 1000 for sure. This is a pretty complex scene with running, jumping, rotating alot. It supported alot like: Fully support all animation states ( climbing, walking, jumping, ragdoll, teleport, death, … ) and pretty customizable if attack, others animation needed. Yes, it has headers. NPCs moves freely, no boundaries or limited range. 0.2 studs precision for X, Z, 0.5 studs precision for Y, 6 degrees precision for Yaw. 20hz sending ( about 0.05 seconds per sent ) Benchmark: 500 NPCs ran at 11KB/s - 12KB/s, ran pretty performant 1000 NPCs ran at 20KB/s - 21KB/s, a bit lag because of using BulkMoveTo , I am thinking of using Motor6D instead. I tried to made this as an open-source replication framework inspiring Lightweight NPCs for everyone to use, but it may takes lots time to make it easily usable tho

items.title: Calculon™ Episode 1 | Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Method for Realistic Camera Shake | (Free + Open Source) {OLD}

posts.author: Devalvion
posts.content: CalculonLogo 490×399 33.4 KB Calculon™ | Episode 1 Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Method for Realistic Camera Shake Table of Contents What is the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Method? Why is math.random not enough? The exact exponential integrator → This is where the differences matter Critically-damped spring post-filter The CFrame composition problem Realistic angle magnitudes formula → Some worked examples Signal pipeline overview Single-axis Rotation - Beginner-friendly Multi-axis Rotation - Advanced → Video Example → Usage examples → Benchmarks Tuning references Closing thoughts References What is the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Method? Leonard Salomon Ornstein (3) 1920×1080 88.3 KB This method originated from two Dutch physicists by the name of Leonard Salomon Ornstein and George Eugene Uhlenbeck in the early 20th century. These two physicists published a model during this time, concluding their studies of the Brownian Motion with Friction , where they attempted to conclude on why particles inside a fluid experience random collisions (noise), and slow down at times due to drag (reversion). Though their method reached a different conclusion, the Brownian Motion experiment concluded that particles experience pure diffusion with no tendency to return to their original state. Though their observations showed that particles slowed down, lost momentum, and got pulled to and fro their origin to reach an equilibrium. With this newfound information, they developed an equation based on Brownian motion in the form of a Stochastic Differential Equation (SDE). With this equation, the world has expanded its use to the maximum, using it in Finance, Biology, Robotics, and even Game Development. The standard model of this equation follows: image (1) 954×127 6.09 KB Why is math.random not enough? Most camera shake implementations you’ll come across on the developer forum will be along the lines of the following: -- Naive random shake (please don't do this) camera.CFrame = camera.CFrame * CFrame.Angles( math.random() * intensity, math.random() * intensity, 0 ) But what’s the problem with this? Each frame teleports the camera to a random location on the screen, completely unrelated, and choppy. Real cameras, whether during a vehicle crash or explosion, have inertia . They wobble, drift, and return to the origin. No random jumps, nothing comes by chance. The mathematically correct way to model something like this is to use the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) Method , which is a mean-reverting stochastic differential equation that is used in physics, finance, and now, your very own Roblox game. The exact exponential integrator The original Euler-Maruyama discretization approximates the original Ornstein-Uhlenbeck SDE using a first-order Taylor expansion. The Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process has a discrete solution derived by solving the Stochastic Differential Equation over one time step. image (4) 968×155 6.27 KB Exact OU step, unconditionally stable for any Δt · Z_n ~ (0,1). The exact stationary variance

items.title: Weekly Recap: May 4-8, 2026

posts.author: Bluff_006
posts.content: Key Takeaways Roblox will discontinue cross-game sales of developer products and passes on May 29 to protect players from fraud and exploitation. You can now create R15 platform avatars with optional joints: articulated fingers, a refined torso with clavicles, and ball-of-foot joints., while Reimport and Asset Privacy Image, Mesh, and Decal assets both hit Full Release. Check out the latest Spring edition of our Creator Roadmap. Read the full recap below! Betas and Tests [Studio Beta] Scene Analysis Tool We’re launching the Scene Analysis tool , a real-time treemap view that lets you quickly identify what’s consuming the most resources in your experience and trace it back to the responsible instances. Flip between modes for script memory, unparented instances, audio memory, animation memory, instance composition, and an extended triangle breakdown to spot performance issues before they reach players. Updates and Announcements Creator Roadmap 2026: Spring Update The Spring Creator Roadmap update shipped 52 items and is adding 21 new items. Read the post for information on what launched, what’s coming, and what’s been delayed. Disabling Cross-Game Sales of Passes and Dev Products and Introducing the Transfers API Roblox will discontinue cross-game sales of developer products and passes on May 29 to protect players from fraud and exploitation. The new Transfers API provides a secure, direct, and immediate path for in-game Robux transfers such as tipping and donations. Creators using the API earn a 10% commission, and recipients receive 90% of the transfer since Roblox takes no platform fee. To send Robux, a Plus subscription is required, and all users under 18 sending or receiving need a linked parental account with approval for every transfer. Full Body Animation for Platform Avatars, Including Fingers You can now create R15 platform avatars with optional joints: articulated fingers, a refined torso with clavicles, and ball-of-foot joints. These are supported by the HumanoidRigDescription and DigitsRigDescription that are part of the Adaptive Animation plugin. Updates to the default animations to include finger motion will be made later this week. [Full Release] Privacy for Newly-Created Image, Mesh, and Decal Assets Privacy for newly created Image, Mesh and Decal assets is now fully launched, adding scalability and quality of life improvements such as bulk sharing and bulk setting to “Open Use”. Asset Privacy will now be enabled by default for newly created individual and group accounts. This does not impact any previously uploaded assets, and can still be enabled/disabled under the account settings. [Full Release] Reimport Update 3D content directly from external files with the new Reimport feature, which preserves instance properties and hierarchies while syncing mesh and texture changes. Right-click a Model , SurfaceAppearance , or Decal in the Explorer to begin. Ads Manager Updates - Maximize Earnings, Attribution Updates, New Tile in Beta, Ot

items.title: [Open-Source] VSV: Native Vertex-Sampled Voxelizer & Noise-Advected Dissolve System

posts.author: Animator_Shanks
posts.content: Hello developers, Working on immersive VFX for Shanks Games requires pushing the engine’s rendering limits without sacrificing performance. I needed a highly optimized way to dissolve complex meshes dynamically based on proximity, but standard particle emitters and tweening simply don’t cut it for complex geometry. Today, I am open-sourcing my solution: the Vertex-Sampled Voxelizer (VSV) . VSV is a volumetric disintegration shader built entirely in Luau. It converts MeshParts into 3D point clouds in real-time, applying noise-advected vector fields to create a cinematic, proximity-based dissolution effect. Key Technical Features Maximum CPU Performance: Built with --!native and --!optimize 2 compilation directives to handle heavy mathematical operations at lightning speed. Vertex UV Extraction: Utilizes EditableMesh and EditableImage to accurately map surface geometry and extract texture pixel colors for every single generated particle. Batched Rendering: Uses Workspace:BulkMoveTo for updating the CFrame of up to 12,000 particles simultaneously while maintaining a stable 60 FPS. Mathematical Culling: The engine automatically stops calculating physics and rendering for particles that are fully dissolved, saving massive amounts of memory. Surface Normal Alignment: Calculates vertex normals via cross products so particles naturally align to the curves and flat surfaces of your custom meshes. Visual Capabilities Custom Particles: Swap out the default voxel blocks with your own custom MeshPart or Part . Perlin Vector Fields: Particles don’t just float; they are driven by a 3D Perlin noise scale, creating a turbulent, ash-like scatter effect. Angular Velocity: Optional randomized rotation spins particles dynamically as they separate from the main mesh body. CFrame Offsetting: Support for pivot offsets and manual CFrame rotation offsets for perfect visual alignment. Showcase Testing Click here to test the Experience Implementation Example The module is designed to be plug-and-play. Place the VSV Module in ReplicatedStorage and call it from a LocalScript : local ReplicatedStorage = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage") local VSV = require(ReplicatedStorage:WaitForChild("VSV")) local targetMesh = workspace:WaitForChild("TargetMesh") local customParticle = ReplicatedStorage:WaitForChild("CustomParticle") local vsvController = VSV.Apply(targetMesh, { MAX_DIST = 40, MIN_DIST = 10, MAX_POINTS = 5000, DOT_SIZE = 0.25, NOISE_SCALE = 0.08, SCATTER_RADIUS = 30, CUSTOM_DOT = customParticle, ALIGN_TO_SURFACE = true, RANDOM_ROTATION = true, DOT_ROTATION_OFFSET = CFrame.Angles(0, math.rad(90), 0) }) -- The effect runs automatically on RenderStepped. -- Call vsvController:Stop() to clean up connections and parts. Get the Module You can grab the uncopylocked package directly from the Creator Store here: Download VSV on the Creator Store The package includes the core module, a detailed README documentation script, and the example LocalScript above. Feel free to use this i

items.title: Calculon™ x @me7474 Episode 5 | BetterTween x Alvionics Labs™ Advanced Tweening for Full-Scale Production | (Free + Open Source) {OLD}

posts.author: Devalvion
posts.content: CalculonLogo 490×399 33.4 KB Calculon™ | Episode 5 BetterTween x Alvionics Labs™ Advanced Tweening for Full-Scale Production In Collaboration with @me7474 ( BetterTween’s author) Copy of Leonard Salomon Ornstein (8) 1920×1080 47.8 KB Table of Contents # Section 1 Background; Tweening in Roblox 2 BetterTween: What it got right; and what breaks 3 The 15+ Motion Models (Episode 5) 4 Module Architecture 5 API Reference → Video Example 6 PhET-Style Simulation 7 Benchmarks 8 Demo Scripts 9 Migration Guide (TweenService / BetterTween) 10 References 1. Background; Tweening in Roblox Every developer who has touched motion in Roblox has met TweenService . It is fast to use, predictable, and correct for a wide class of problems; doors, UI fades, cinematic paths. For those cases it remains the right tool. This post is not an argument against TweenService; it is an argument that TweenService has a well-defined boundary, and once you cross that boundary, reaching for it anyway causes real, observable bugs. The boundary is state. A tween prescribes a position as a function of time: x(t) . It stores nothing else. The moment velocity matters; camera smoothing, recoil, spring bones, hover systems; position-only thinking becomes incomplete. The moment you need a different motion law rather than a different easing curve; Bézier curves, Catmull-Rom splines, constant-speed traversal, jerk-limited S-profiles; TweenService offers no path forward. You write it yourself or reach for a community module. Why dynamics go further than easing. Episode 4 established the mathematical case for spring-mass-damper systems as a superior camera/follow/recoil primitive. Episode 5 extends this into a unified module that covers the full spectrum: from simple lerp to exact ODE springs, from quadratic Bézier to arc-length-reparameterized Catmull-Rom splines, from smoothstep to jerk-limited 7-segment S-curves. Every model is mathematically documented, frame-rate independent, and zero-replication by design. Design principle: Zero network bandwidth impact. All systems are client-local by default. Because the exact spring integrator is deterministic, you can replicate just the initial state (pos, vel, wn, zeta, target) and each client produces an identical trajectory. 2. BetterTween: What it got right; and what breaks This section is written with full respect for @me7474 ’s work. BetterTween was a genuine innovation when it was released in June 2023, and it inspired the architecture of Episode 5. The author himself acknowledges the flaws in the post header: “this module was made a long time ago and has quite a few flaws.” Episode 5 is the answer to what comes next. The source code (114 lines, github.com/me7474/BetterTween ) is compact and readable. Here is a thorough technical audit. What BetterTween Got Right 1. The core idea is correct. Driving a NumberValue through TweenService, then using its interpolated Value to lerp a Part’s CFrame each frame, is a clever workaround for TweenService’s

items.title: Evolving Luau OSS: Community Contributions & More!

posts.author: designerwithlonghair
posts.content: Key Takeaways We’re rolling out open-source community contributions and Wally / Creator Store publishing for our newest Luau repositories, including our new Signals library, and open-sourcing our CLI for running locally-developed Luau projects on Open Cloud . Hello creators! We’re excited to announce today that we’re pushing forward the state of Open Source Luau at Roblox. We have several exciting new announcements to share, so let’s dive right in without further ado! Make direct impact with Community Contributions 1600×500 919 KB In previous years, Roblox’s open source Luau libraries have only been source available , meaning we only publish their source code. But that changes today - you are all among the most creative communities in the world, building tools that deliver incredible value, so we’re passionate about bringing you closer to our process as much as internal bandwidth allows us. For the first time, we will be rolling out Community Contributions for select Luau libraries ! We’ve been hard at work building a process that allows external contributors to make changes directly to some of our newest Luau repositories. When a Luau library accepts community contributions, it’ll show up on our GitHub as a completely standard GitHub repository, like any other you would see elsewhere. Scroll down to the readme, and look for the blue badge and the open-source cube: 694×164 3.6 KB These repositories are not a read-only mirror - they are the source of truth for these codebases. You will be able to open issues and pull requests against these libraries just like any other, and you’ll get to work with Roblox employees on the open source projects you’re passionate about. We’ve invested in a lot of tooling to ensure that external contributors have an equal experience when contributing to these open-source projects. All testing, CI, linting and formatting are built on 100% public tooling you can adopt too . That means anyone who wants to contribute can get set up with a full toolchain and easily iterate on their changes without having to rely on other people. We even built a new tool that we’re open-sourcing today - more on that later In addition, we also have all-new deployment features that bring your favourite packages to even more places than ever before. Roblox libraries using Community Contributions can now publish to Wally under the roblox/ namespace . We also built a new packaging workflow that collects all the required dependencies together into a single Roblox model, so that libraries are available and ready-for-use via the Creator Store . We’re excited to usher in this next-generation version of our open-source vision - and what better way to do that than to release a brand-new open source library? Signals - Modern, Open-Source Reactive State Our very first library to adopt Community Contributions is Signals, a scalable and minimal reactive programming library built by Roblox for high-performance state management. Signals is a modern alternati

posts.author: PysephDEV
posts.content: Really cool to see the community contributions model and Signals in particular - the pull-based lazy evaluation approach is a solid choice for game state, and drawing from Fusion/S.js is encouraging. That said, I want to bring up something that’s been on my mind for a while regarding the broader React-Lua story. I understand why Roblox adopted React internally; it makes onboarding engineers from web backgrounds significantly easier, and there’s a massive existing ecosystem of patterns and knowledge to draw from. But from the perspective of someone actually building on the platform, React-Lua has always felt like a bit of a square peg in a round hole. The core issue is performance at the boundary between React’s reconciliation model and Roblox’s DataModel. Even today, the built-in React-Lua UIs (chat, player list) can produce frame spikes upwards of 5ms - and this is after Roblox’s own stated efforts to address it. My concern is that this isn’t really a fixable bug, it’s more of a fundamental architectural mismatch. A single property read on a Roblox Instance can cost half a millisecond in bad cases; now multiply that across hundreds of reads in a single frame when React triggers re-renders or propagates updates down through sub-components, and you’ve got a recipe for consistent hitching that no amount of optimization will fully solve. Signals looks like it could help on the state management side of things, but I’m curious whether the team has looked at the React-Lua rendering layer itself with the same critical eye. Is there appetite internally to explore UI paradigms that are more native to how Roblox’s engine actually works, rather than adapting patterns built around a browser DOM that doesn’t have the same per-read costs? Would love to hear the team’s thoughts on this - sorry if this is somewhat off-topic, but it felt relevant given react-lua also being maintained by Roblox.

However, Signals has been used to great effect internally to speed up some of Roblox’s heavier UIs by providing an ergonomic granular-reactive alternative to React’s diffing model when dealing with bound state. It doesn’t go through the same foundational mechanisms as React’s usual state does, so you should find it alleviates some of the performance pressures you’d normally find with React. Feel free to play around with Signals and see if it helps alleviate some of the performance concerns you’ve seen - we’re excited to invest in making sure our frameworks are as performant as can be

items.title: Calculon™ Episode 4 | Dynamics Beyond Interpolation using Post-Grad Mathematics | (Free + Open Source) {OLD}

posts.author: Devalvion
posts.content: CalculonLogo 490×399 33.4 KB Calculon™ | Episode 4 Dynamics Beyond Interpolation using Post-Grad Mathematics Table of Contents What this episode is about Why interpolation is not motion PhET interactive simulation Position space vs Phase space Differential equations as motion laws Tweening vs Governing laws Easing is not physics (C0/C1/C2) Spring mass damper deep dive Energy, stability why motion explodes Numerical integration in Roblox DynamicMotion; The full module Video example Preset library Folder tree Studio setup Implementation guides visualizers Benchmarks Limitations Closing thoughts References What this episode is about If you have spent any time writing motion systems in Roblox, you have reached for TweenService , :Lerp() , or an easing curve. These tools are not wrong. For doors, UI fades, cinematic paths, and deterministic animations, they are the correct choice: fast to author, predictable in output, and zero runtime overhead beyond a per-frame interpolation. This episode is not about replacing them everywhere. It is about identifying the boundary where they stop being enough, and explaining precisely what goes wrong when you push past that boundary without changing your mental model. That boundary is state . An interpolation function tells an object where to be. It does not tell the object how fast it is moving when it arrives, whether it has momentum, whether there is a force acting on it, or what happens if the destination changes mid-flight. A dynamic system does all of that automatically, because it does not prescribe positions over time; it prescribes laws , and the positions follow. CORE CLAIM The moment velocity matters, position-only thinking becomes incomplete. The moment acceleration matters, easing becomes insufficient. The moment stability matters, motion becomes mathematics. This episode builds a complete second-order dynamical system library for Roblox, grounded in classical mechanics. The module is over 1.1k lines with full documentation, three damping regime integrators (underdamped, critically damped, overdamped), energy tracking, C0/C1/C2 continuity analysis, frame-rate independence tests, and a preset library of 13 pre-tuned configurations. Three standalone visualizer LocalScripts demonstrate the concepts directly in-engine with live displacement graphs. Why interpolation is not motion A tween defines a function: Position Prescription x(t); position is a function of time alone This is a parametric path . Time is the input; position is the output. Nothing else is stored. The representation does not contain: WHAT INTERPOLATION DISCARDS velocity · acceleration · force · mass · damping · momentum · energy · constraints · stability The consequences are concrete. Consider two objects at the same position: The State Identification Problem Object A → x = 10, v = 0; stationary Object B → x = 10, v = 100; moving at 100 studs/s A position-only system sees A and B as identical. A dynamic system treats them as entirely differ

items.title: Rogen | Feature-Based Folder Structures with Rojo | An Automatic Tool for Luau and Roblox-ts

posts.author: Clinkety
posts.content: Rogen A tool for feature-based folder structures with Rojo image 701×781 134 KB What is Rogen? Rogen is a command line tool that brings feature-based architecture to Roblox development for both luau and roblox-ts. Instead of separating your codebase in a client , shared and server folder at the root level, Rogen lets you group your code by domain and feature. You can keep your inventory UI, inventory server script, and inventory client script all inside a single inventory folder. This eliminates context-switching across different folders, making your codebase significantly easier to navigate, refactor, and scale. In the background, Rogen watches your file system and dynamically generates your default.project.json map for Rojo. You get the freedom to group your code in any way you want, and Rogen takes care of sorting everything into the correct Roblox services like ReplicatedStorage and ServerScriptService . Moreover, Rogen allows you to merge multiple directories into a single Rojo project. This is useful for multi-place games where you want to share a core across different places. Note: If you use luau, it is highly recommended to set up darklua for improved string requires. Automatic Routing Rogen determines where a file belongs by looking at your folder structure, hidden marker files, and file names. When multiple rules apply to the same file, Rogen follows a simple principle: the most specific instruction wins. An explicit rule placed directly on a file will always override a general rule set by its parent folder. Here are the routing strategies, listed from lowest to highest priority: 1. Folder Name If a folder is named after a routing keyword ( server , client , shared ) or a Roblox service (e.g., ReplicatedFirst ), all files within it inherit that destination. Behavior: Rogen consumes the routing keyword and strips it from the final generated path. Example: src/combat/client/combatController.luau becomes StarterPlayerScripts/client/combat/combatController.luau . 2. Marker File To route a folder, you can also place an empty marker file (e.g., .server , .client , .shared ) directly inside the directory. Behavior: The entire folder is routed to that service, but the folder’s name is preserved in the Roblox tree. Example: A folder named AntiCheat containing a .server marker file will be routed to ServerScriptService/server/AntiCheat . 3. File Name To route a specific file differently than its parent folder, use a routing prefix or suffix. File affixes are absolute and will always override folder names and marker files. Delimited: Use a separator (dot, hyphen, or underscore) before or after the base name. Examples: input-client.ts, server.data.ts, CamelCase PascalCase: Prepend or append the mapped keyword directly to the filename. Examples: inputClient.ts, serverData.ts Note: By default, Rogen strips the routing keyword from the final module name (e.g., serverData.ts and data.server.ts become Data and data , respectively). You can disable this
